raw_consumer_statement:
Hi, on XX/XX/year>, a scammer hacked into my Macy 's online account and purchased a {$10.00} fragrance sampler and a {$150.00} cologne, which were both sent to an address in Florida. Citibank credited me the {$10.00} but denied the {$150.00} charge, even though both of those purchases were made at the same time and sent to the same address in Florida. I have called four times only to get the run around. The original customer service representative ( I believe due to XXXX not being the representative 's first language ) incorrectly treated this transaction as goods that I ordered but never received although I clearly and repeatedly told her that this was a fraudulent transaction. Let me be clear : I, nor did anyone I authorized to use my account, ordered this merchandise. This is clearly fraud but I have not been able to get a favorable resolution and the customer representatives have been no help and have instead issued me two new credit cards although there is no ongoing fraud on my account after the fraudulent transaction on XX/XX/XXXX. The {$10.00} charge posted on XX/XX/XXXX and the {$150.00} charge posted on XX/XX/XXXX. I have never in my life had this much trouble in getting a fraudulent charge reversed. Any assistance you can provided would be greatly appreciated. 

Thank you, XXXX XXXX
